statement.markdown: Write a function that takes a string as input and returns the reversed string. However, the function contains a logical bug that makes it inefficient for large inputs. Your task is to fix the bug and optimize the function.
statement.inputFormat: A string s of length up to 10000 characters
statement.notes: The function should work in O(n) time complexity, where n is the length of the input string.

hints.text: Use a more efficient algorithm to reverse the string, such as using a two-pointer approach

editorial.title: Optimizing String Reversal
editorial.intuition: The naive approach to reversing a string is to use a loop that iterates over the string and appends each character to a new string. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n^2) due to the string concatenation. A more efficient approach is to use a two-pointer technique, where we iterate over the string from both ends and swap the characters at the corresponding indices.
editorial.bruteForce: The naive approach to reversing a string is to use a loop that iterates over the string and appends each character to a new string.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a two-pointer technique, where we iterate over the string from both ends and swap the characters at the corresponding indices.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a two-pointer technique, where we iterate over the string from both ends and swap the characters at the corresponding indices. This approach has a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The two-pointer technique correctly reverses the string by swapping the characters at the corresponding indices.
editorial.dryRun: Let's dry run the two-pointer technique on the input string "Hello, World!". We start with two pointers, one at the beginning of the string and one at the end. We then swap the characters at the corresponding indices until the pointers meet in the middle.
commonMistakes: Using a naive approach with string concatenation
